title: ceratocephala falcata subsp. orthoceras
intro: Ceratocephala falcata subsp. orthoceras, also known as the toothwort or Alpine toothwort, is a flowering plant species in the genus Ceratocephala, characterized by its distinctive tooth-shaped leaves and yellow or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Ceratocephala falcata subsp. orthoceras are thought to include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
